title: Longest Increasing Subsequence
description: Given an array of integers nums, return the length of the longest increasing subsequence. A subsequence is a sequence that can be derived from another sequence by deleting some elements without changing the order of the remaining elements.

examples.explanation: The longest increasing subsequence is [2, 3, 7, 101], which has a length of 4.
The longest increasing subsequence is [0, 1, 2, 3], which has a length of 4.
All elements are the same, so the longest increasing subsequence can only be one element long, resulting in a length of 1.

constraints: The length of nums will be between 1 and 2500.
Each element in nums will be between -10^4 and 10^4.
hints: Consider maintaining an array to keep track of the lengths of increasing subsequences.
You may want to use binary search to optimize your solution.
Think about how to handle elements that are equal to the current maximum in your increasing subsequence.
Try to come up with a solution that works in O(n log n) time complexity.
